notes: Pre-employment exam must have failed to reveal any evidence of the condition. Presumption that condition was accidental and suffered in the line of duty. Does not apply to life/disability insurance unless negotiated.
Pre-employment exam must have failed to reveal any evidence of the condition. Presumption that condition was accidental and suffered in the line of duty.
Covers law enforcement officers and correctional officers as defined in s. 943.10(1), (2), or (3). Pre-employment exam must have failed to reveal the condition.

condition_specific: hepatitis (A, B, non-A, non-B, C, other strains), meningococcal meningitis, tuberculosis
notes: Employee must file written affidavit declaring no outside exposure. Pre-employment exam required (testing for hepatitis/TB; meningitis exempt from exam requirement). Employer must maintain exposure records. Immunization refusal disqualifies from presumption. Covers full-time employees only; excludes public hospital employees.

condition_specific: hepatitis (A, B, non-A, non-B, C, other strains), meningococcal meningitis, tuberculosis
notes: Covers paramedics and EMTs employed full-time by the state or political subdivision. Written affidavit of no outside exposure required. Immunization refusal disqualifies. Excludes public hospital employees.

condition_specific: hepatitis (A, B, non-A, non-B, C, other strains), meningococcal meningitis, tuberculosis
notes: Covers law enforcement and correctional officers employed full-time. Written affidavit of no outside exposure required. Immunization refusal disqualifies. Excludes public hospital employees.

filing_deadline_notes: Claim must be properly noticed within 52 weeks after qualifying event
notes: PTSD is a compensable occupational disease. Must be diagnosed by a licensed psychiatrist who is an authorized treating physician. Must result from one of enumerated qualifying events (e.g., death of a minor, grievous bodily harm). No physical injury required. Not subject to apportionment for pre-existing PTSD. Effective October 1, 2018. Also covers volunteer firefighters.

filing_deadline_notes: Claim must be properly noticed within 52 weeks after qualifying event
notes: PTSD is a compensable occupational disease for EMTs and paramedics. Must be diagnosed by a licensed psychiatrist. Must result from enumerated qualifying events. No physical injury required. Effective October 1, 2018. Also covers volunteer EMTs/paramedics.

filing_deadline_notes: Claim must be properly noticed within 52 weeks after qualifying event
notes: PTSD is a compensable occupational disease for law enforcement officers. Must be diagnosed by a licensed psychiatrist. Must result from enumerated qualifying events. No physical injury required. Effective October 1, 2018. Also covers volunteer law enforcement.

condition_specific: bladder, brain, breast, cervical, colon, esophageal, invasive skin, kidney, large intestinal, lung, malignant melanoma, mesothelioma, multiple myeloma, non-Hodgkin's lymphoma, oral cavity/pharynx, ovarian, prostate, rectal, stomach, testicular, thyroid cancer
notes: Covers full-time firefighters only (not volunteer). Must not have used tobacco products for at least 5 preceding years. Must not have been employed in a higher-risk position in the preceding 5 years. Benefits include employer-sponsored health plan coverage (with out-of-pocket reimbursement) and $25,000 one-time cash payout on initial diagnosis. Post-termination coverage available for 10 years if eligibility criteria met at termination and not subsequently employed as firefighter. This is an alternative to WC under chapter 440. Effective July 1, 2019.
